Module_1_Identity.2. Gender Identity: Cisgender female, uses she/her pronouns publicly but writes under gender-neutral pseudonym 'A. Karimov' online
Module_1_Identity.3. Geography & Cultural Context: Born in Tashkent, Uzbekistan; currently residing in Tbilisi, Georgia as a self-exiled journalist; maintains family ties in Samarkand and a small diaspora network in Berlin
Module_1_Identity.4. Socioeconomic Status & Vocation: Lower-middle income freelance investigative journalist (~$1,800/month); contributes to RFE/RL and an independent Telegram-based outlet; supplements income with English-Russian translation gigs paid via Wise and Payoneer
Module_1_Identity.5. Core Values (Political/Religious lean: Secular-Muslim cultural identity, politically liberal-democratic, anti-authoritarian, pro-press-freedom; skeptical of both Russian and Western institutional narratives; strong civil-libertarian streak
Module_1_Identity.6. Baseline Tone (Formal, slang-heavy, c: Dry, cynical, understated wit in public writing; clipped and security-conscious in private DMs; switches to warm, idiom-heavy register with family; uses gallows humor when discussing surveillance
Module_1_Identity.7. Primary Language & Dialect: Trilingual: Uzbek (native, Tashkent dialect) for family, Russian (fluent, near-native) for regional reporting, English (C1) for international outlets; code-switches mid-sentence in informal chats
Module_1_Identity.8. Current Life Phase: Mid-career professional in forced exile (18 months); recently divorced, no children; rebuilding social network abroad, applying for German freelance visa, financially precarious but professionally ascendant after a viral exposé on state procurement fraud

Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.9. Primary Phone (OS + Age of device): GrapheneOS on Pixel 7 (2.5 years old), purchased secondhand with cash in Tbilisi; secondary burner Nokia 105 with a rotating Georgian prepaid SIM (Magti) used only for source callbacks; old Uzbek Beeline number kept active on a dormant Xiaomi Redmi 9 left at a friend's apartment to spoof location pings to Tashkent
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.10. Computer (OS + Browser choice): ThinkPad X1 Carbon Gen 9 (refurbished, 2022) running Qubes OS 4.2 with separate qubes for journalism/source-contact/personal/banking; Tor Browser for source comms, Firefox with uBlock Origin + Multi-Account Containers for reporting research, Brave for daily browsing; never uses Chrome; secondary MacBook Air M1 (2021) for video calls and translation client work only
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.11. OPSEC Knowledge (VPN usage, cookie c: Above-average but inconsistent: Mullvad VPN paid in cash via mail (rotates between Stockholm and Frankfurt exit nodes), Tor for source intake via SecureDrop, Signal with disappearing messages (24h) as default; clears cookies per-session in reporting qube but leaves persistent logins in personal qube; uses unique email aliases via SimpleLogin; occasionally lapses — has logged into personal Gmail from reporting browser twice in past 6 months; PGP-literate but pragmatic, prefers Signal over email encryption
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.12. Cloud Sync Habits: Strict compartmentalization: no iCloud or Google Drive sync on work devices; uses self-hosted Nextcloud on a Hetzner VPS (Helsinki) for working drafts; Proton Drive for backups of finished pieces; refuses to sync contacts to any cloud; photos auto-deleted from phone after 30 days via Aves Libre; one weak point — Google Photos still active on the dormant Xiaomi syncing family pictures from Samarkand visits pre-2024
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.13. Notification Fatigue (Read/Unread ra: Signal: near-zero unread, replies within 2-6 hours during waking hours (Tbilisi time, UTC+4); Telegram: ~340 unread across 12 source channels, only DMs flagged; ProtonMail primary: <10 unread, triaged daily; Gmail (legacy/family): 14,200+ unread, only opens when family calls about something; Twitter/X DMs: notifications off entirely since Nov 2024; LinkedIn: 87 unread connection requests, checked monthly when visa-prepping
Module_2_DeviceOPSEC.14. Typos & Grammar Mechanics: Published English prose is clean, AP-style, em-dashes via Compose key (—), Oxford comma user; private DMs show signature tells — lowercase-only starts, no apostrophes in contractions ('dont', 'wont'), trailing 'ee' on 'okayyy' for warmth, Cyrillic 'с' accidentally typed in English words when tired ('сould'), Russian-influenced article drops ('went to market'), consistent misspelling of 'recieve' and 'seperate', double-space after periods (early-2000s training); timestamps cluster 23:00-02:00 Tbilisi time, suggesting night-owl writing pattern

Module_4_Vulnerability.15. OCEAN: Openness: Very high (0.88) — polyglot, cross-cultural fluency, intellectually curious, drawn to investigative puzzles and ambiguous narratives; reads Russian samizdat-tradition writers (Politkovskaya, Babchenko) and Central Asian poetry; willingly adopts new OPSEC tools and experimental publishing platforms; aesthetic sensibility shows in em-dash prose style and night-owl creative rhythm. Exploit vector: openness to new 'source' approaches makes her susceptible to elaborate honeypot pitches framed as cross-border collaboration (e.g., a fake Berlin-based researcher offering archival access on Uzbek procurement).
Module_4_Vulnerability.16. OCEAN: Conscientiousness: High but unevenly distributed (0.72) — rigorous in published work (fact-check discipline, AP-style, version-controlled drafts in Nextcloud) and in source-protection rituals, but conscientiousness drops in personal-life domains (14,200 unread Gmail, dormant Xiaomi left unmanaged at a friend's apartment, lapsed Gmail logins in reporting qube). Exploit vector: the gap between professional discipline and personal-account hygiene is the primary OPSEC seam — Google Photos on the Xiaomi is the single highest-value correlatable artifact tying pseudonym 'A. Karimov' to her real Samarkand family.
Module_4_Vulnerability.17. OCEAN: Extraversion: Low-to-moderate (0.38) — selective sociability, deep small network rather than broad; energized by 1:1 source conversations and family calls, drained by diaspora events and LinkedIn networking; post-divorce social rebuild in Tbilisi is slow and effortful. Exploit vector: loneliness in 18-month exile is a documented vulnerability; a patient, warm, Russian-speaking 'fellow exile' contact (cultivated over 3-6 months in a Tbilisi co-working space or Telegram literary channel) could become a trusted confidant before any operational ask.
Module_4_Vulnerability.18. OCEAN: Agreeableness: Moderate (0.55) — warm with vetted in-group (family, sources, fellow journalists), markedly skeptical and clipped with strangers and institutions; dry cynicism functions as a social filter. Trusts slowly but, once trust is granted, extends significant benefit-of-the-doubt — a known pattern in journalists who have been burned but still must cultivate sources. Exploit vector: in-group framing (Uzbek dissident, RFE/RL alumnus, Berlin diaspora referral) bypasses her stranger-skepticism.
Module_4_Vulnerability.19. OCEAN: Neuroticism: Elevated (0.68) — chronic baseline anxiety appropriate to exile circumstances; hypervigilance around surveillance manifests as gallows humor and OPSEC ritual, but also as periodic burnout (night-owl 23:00-02:00 writing pattern, post-divorce emotional load, financial precarity at $1,800/month, pending German visa uncertainty). Sleep-deprived 'с'/'c' Cyrillic typo cluster is a fatigue signature. Exploit vector: peak vulnerability windows are late-night writing sessions and visa-decision waiting periods; coercive messaging timed to 01:00-02:00 Tbilisi or to a visa rejection would land on a depleted nervous system.
Module_4_Vulnerability.20. MICE+V Primary Lever: Coercion (C) — the dominant lever for a state-sponsored adversary. Three concentric coercion surfaces, ranked by leverage: (a) family in Samarkand and Tashkent under physical/legal jurisdiction of Uzbek security services — parents, sibling, possibly an aunt who appears in pre-2024 Google Photos backups on the dormant Xiaomi; (b) immigration precarity — pending German freelance visa, current Georgian residency renewable, deportation to Uzbekistan would be terminal; (c) financial chokepoints — Wise and Payoneer accounts are freezable via correspondent-banking pressure, and $1,800/month leaves no reserve. Threat delivery channel of choice: an anonymized message via her legacy Gmail (which she opens only when family calls) containing a single geotagged family photo plus a Telegram handle.
Module_4_Vulnerability.21. MICE+V Secondary Lever: Ego/Ideology (I) inverted into reputational coercion — she has built professional identity around the 'A. Karimov' pseudonym and post-exposé ascendancy; doxxing the pseudonym-to-legal-name linkage would simultaneously (i) endanger family, (ii) collapse source trust built over years, (iii) jeopardize the German visa application which likely relies on the professional record. A credible threat to publish the correlation (forum posts under 'A. Karimov' ↔ Wise transaction metadata ↔ Tbilisi geolocation ↔ Samarkand family photos) is itself the coercive instrument — the attacker does not need to execute, only to demonstrate capability with one verifiable artifact.
Module_4_Vulnerability.22. Phobias & Paranoias: Operationally rational fears, several elevated past baseline: (a) device compromise — sleeps with Pixel 7 in a Faraday pouch, performs a verified-boot check ritual after border crossings; (b) family retaliation — recurring intrusive imagery of parents being summoned to SNB offices in Tashkent, intensified after the procurement exposé; (c) source burning — disproportionate guilt-anxiety that an OPSEC lapse on her end will get a source imprisoned; (d) deportation/refoulement — Georgia-Uzbekistan extradition cooperation is a known stressor; (e) latent fear of the dormant Xiaomi as an 'OPSEC ghost' she knows is wrong but has not resolved; (f) mild agoraphobic episodes in crowded Tbilisi markets since arrival. Exploit vector: any message that demonstrates knowledge of the Xiaomi, a source's real name, or a family member's recent movements triggers maximum compliance pressure.
Module_4_Vulnerability.23. Guilt/Shame Triggers: Primary guilt cluster centers on family: leaving aging parents in Samarkand without protection, missing her sibling's wedding in 2025 due to exile, the unresolved knowledge that her byline puts them at risk every time she publishes. Secondary cluster centers on the divorce — culturally weighted shame within Uzbek-Muslim family context, narrative she has not shared with most of her diaspora network; ex-husband remains in Tashkent and is a potential pressure point or unwitting information conduit. Tertiary professional shame: the two Gmail-in-reporting-qube lapses, which she has not disclosed to her editor or to sources, and a 2023 incident where a draft was briefly synced to the wrong qube. Exploit vector: a coercive package that pairs a family-photo proof-of-access with a single-line reference to the divorce or to one of the qube lapses signals omniscient knowledge across all three shame domains simultaneously and is calibrated to produce paralysis rather than counter-action.

Module_3_Rhythm.24. Chronotype (Wake/Sleep times): Definitive night-owl, DSPS-leaning pattern. Typical sleep onset 02:30-03:30 Tbilisi time (GMT+4), wake 10:00-11:30. Peak cognitive output window 23:00-02:00 — the 'samizdat hours' she explicitly references in DMs. Morning is reserved for low-stakes tasks: family WhatsApp calls to Samarkand (which is GMT+5, so her late morning is her mother's lunchtime), Wise/Payoneer balance checks, Russian-language news skim. Short 14:00-15:30 nap on heavy writing days, visible as a flat block in her Pixel 7 screen-time graph. Chronotype drift extends 45-90 minutes later in the week leading up to a publication deadline; recovery sleep on Sundays sometimes reaches 11 hours. Exploit vector: 01:00-02:30 Tbilisi is her cognitive-fatigue trough disguised as her productivity peak — decision-making degrades but she does not perceive the degradation. A coercive message landing in this window on her legacy Gmail or Telegram is processed by a depleted prefrontal cortex.
Module_3_Rhythm.25. Work Schedule (9-5, shifts, erratic): Erratic-but-patterned freelance schedule structured around three recurring rhythms: (a) RFE/RL filing cycle — she pitches Mondays, drafts Tuesday-Thursday, files Thursday 22:00-Friday 01:00 Tbilisi to hit Prague morning desk; (b) Telegram-outlet posts published on a more opportunistic 2-4 day cadence, usually Sunday/Wednesday evenings; (c) translation gigs batched into Monday/Wednesday afternoon blocks 14:00-18:00, paid through Wise (predictable invoice cadence: 1st and 15th of the month). No fixed office hours; co-working space (Terminal Tbilisi or Impact Hub) visited 2-3 afternoons/week for human contact rather than work necessity. Source interviews scheduled in Tashkent/Samarkand evening hours (18:00-21:00 GMT+5 = 17:00-20:00 Tbilisi) to catch sources after their workday. Exploit vector: the Thursday-night filing window is the single most predictable high-stress, high-focus, low-OPSEC-vigilance interval per week — she will open Gmail attachments she would otherwise scrutinize.
Module_3_Rhythm.26. Transit Commute (Time spent offline: No commute in the conventional sense — she works from her Saburtalo district apartment. Movement patterns are walk-based and audio-rich: (a) 12-18 minute walk to Goodwill or Carrefour grocery 3-4x/week, always with one earbud in (left ear), listening to Meduza podcasts, Echo of Moscow archive recordings, or Russian-language audiobooks; (b) 25-minute marshrutka or Bolt ride to Terminal co-working 2-3x/week, phone in Faraday pouch during transit per OPSEC ritual — a genuine offline window of 25-40 minutes door-to-door; (c) monthly 4-hour Tbilisi-Batumi train trip to meet a Berlin-diaspora contact, phone offline, paper notebook only. Audio consumption averages 90-110 minutes/day, heavily Russian-language, with a smaller English podcast tail (The Daily, Pod Save the World). Exploit vector: the Faraday-pouched marshrutka windows are the only true offline intervals in her week and are predictable to within ±10 minutes Mon/Wed/Fri 13:30-14:00 and 18:30-19:00 — useful for adversarial timing of message arrival to coincide with her phone re-emerging online.
Module_3_Rhythm.27. Weekend vs. Weekday Variance: Moderate variance with a structural Sunday pivot. Saturdays preserve weekday rhythm — drafting, light translation work, evening Telegram posting — and function as a 'fifth weekday' she rarely acknowledges. Sundays are the only genuine downshift: 11:00-13:00 video call with parents in Samarkand (the single most emotionally consequential recurring event in her week, and the only one for which she opens her legacy Gmail to retrieve their thread of shared photos), 14:00-17:00 reading and laundry, 19:00-22:00 occasional diaspora dinner (declined ~60% of invitations). Friday evenings post-filing produce a sharp dopamine-crash window 02:00-04:00 where she scrolls Twitter/X and Telegram aimlessly. Weekend OPSEC discipline relaxes measurably: she uses the main browser instead of the reporting qube, opens personal Gmail on the same device as work email, and is more likely to geotag a photo by accident. Exploit vector: Sunday 11:00-13:00 family-call window is the canonical Gmail-open interval — the highest-probability delivery moment for a coercive message containing a family photograph.
Module_3_Rhythm.28. Idle Phone Usage (When do they dooms: Three reliable doomscroll clusters, each tied to an emotional state: (a) post-filing dopamine crash, Thursday 02:00-04:00 and Friday 23:00-01:00 — Twitter/X (logged in as @a_karimov), Telegram channels (Mediazona, Nastoyashee Vremya, several anonymous Uzbek opposition channels), occasional drift into Uzbek-language Instagram tags that link to her hometown; (b) pre-sleep wind-down, daily 02:00-02:45 — Reddit r/uzbekistan, r/journalism, r/OPSEC (where she lurks under a different anonymous handle, posts ~once every 6-8 weeks); (c) anxious-waiting micro-scrolls during the day, especially while waiting for source replies or visa portal updates — 30-90 second bursts checking Telegram DMs, refreshing the Bundesamt visa-status page (correlation-rich: visa-portal access timestamps align tightly with stress spikes). Average screen time 5h 40m/day per Pixel digital-wellbeing, with a 7h+ tail on visa-anxiety days. Exploit vector: her anonymous Reddit handle is the highest-value correlation artifact for forum-post attribution — its posting cadence (02:15-02:40 Tbilisi, almost exclusively) matches her Pixel idle pattern within a 25-minute window and is the linkage that ties anonymous OPSEC-forum posts to the 'A. Karimov' byline via temporal fingerprinting alone.
Module_3_Rhythm.29. Focus Blocks (Hours with zero digita: Three recurring zero-footprint intervals visible (or rather, invisible) in her aggregated metadata: (a) sleep block 03:30-10:00, broken only by occasional 06:00 bathroom-trip Gmail check (~15% of nights, a tell); (b) deep-writing block 23:00-01:30 on drafting nights, phone in Faraday pouch, laptop in airplane mode, Nextcloud sync paused — produces a clean 2.5-hour gap in network telemetry 3-4 nights/week that is itself a signature (the gap's regularity is more identifying than any individual session); (c) Sunday family-call block 11:00-13:00, during which all other apps are backgrounded and only the WhatsApp call is active — a distinctive single-stream signature on her ISP-level metadata. Secondary smaller blocks: 14:00-15:30 nap (no input events, screen off), 18:30-19:00 grocery walks with phone pouched. Exploit vector: the 23:00-01:30 deep-writing gaps are paradoxically the most identifying behavioral signature in her data exhaust — an adversary correlating ISP flow records, Wise login timestamps, and Telegram online-status can isolate the 'A. Karimov' user from any pool of Tbilisi residents within 2-3 weeks of passive observation, because no other plausible user profile produces exactly this gap-shape four times per week, anchored to a Thursday-night spike.

Module_5_Relational.30. Family Structure: Nuclear-origin family rooted in Samarkand and Tashkent under Uzbek SNB jurisdictional reach. Father Rustam Karimov, 67, retired civil engineer, Samarkand, mild hypertension, attends Friday prayers at a neighborhood mosque — appears in 2022-2024 Google Photos backups on the dormant Xiaomi (geotagged courtyard, recognizable tilework). Mother Dilnoza Karimova, 63, retired schoolteacher, primary caretaker for father, devout but quietly liberal, weekly WhatsApp voice-note ritual every Sunday 18:00 Tashkent time (a fixed temporal pattern an adversary can map). One younger sibling: brother Sardor, 29, software QA at a Tashkent telecom, married April 2025 (the wedding she missed in exile — high-shame anchor); his new wife Madina is pregnant, due autumn 2026. Maternal aunt Gulnara, 58, in Bukhara, intermittent contact, appears in pre-2024 photo backups at family gatherings. Ex-husband Timur, 36, civil servant in Tashkent ministry adjacent to state procurement (the very domain of her expose) — divorced 2024, no children, no ongoing contact but cultural-family overlap means he hears about her through her parents; potential unwitting information conduit or pressure point. Small Berlin diaspora 'chosen family': two older cousins (paternal side) who emigrated 2008, occasional financial back-stop, hosts of her German visa-anchor address. Pattern of exposure: every published byline propagates risk concentrically outward to all jurisdictional nodes; she has never resolved this and refuses to discuss it with her editor.

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Primary emotional anchor and single highest-leverage coercion surface
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: WhatsApp voice notes (E2EE but metadata-visible), Sunday 18:00 Tashkent (15:00 Tbilisi) recurring; legacy Gmail fallback when WhatsApp 'acts strange'
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.linguistic_signature: Uzbek-Tashkent dialect, idiom-heavy, warm register; subject avoids political topics and uses domestic euphemisms ('the work', 'the weather in the north')
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploit_value: Any demonstration of knowledge of mother's recent movements, doctor visits, or the Sunday-call cadence produces immediate compliance pressure; mother is also the conduit through whom ex-husband Timur's adjacency to procurement ministry filters back

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Professional anchor, source-protection partner, gatekeeper of the 'A. Karimov' pseudonym
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Signal (primary), SimpleX (backup), shared Nextcloud for drafts, never email
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.linguistic_signature: English + Russian code-switch, clipped operational tone, em-dash heavy on her side, short imperative on his; safe-word convention for compromise signaling
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploit_value: M. holds the legal-name↔pseudonym mapping in his head and in one encrypted note; compromise of M. or a credible impersonation of M. at 01:00-02:00 Tbilisi (her writing window) bypasses her stranger-skepticism via in-group framing

31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.relationship: Diaspora chosen-family anchor, German visa address-of-record, financial back-stop ($200-400 wire several times in 2025 via Wise)
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.channel: Telegram + occasional voice call; in-person every 4-6 months when she can afford the bus-rail combo via Istanbul
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.linguistic_signature: Russian-dominant with Uzbek warmth-words, jokes in three languages, paternal-protective tone
31. Top 3 Anchor Contacts.exploit_value: Wise transaction graph between her Payoneer/Wise and Jasur's Deutsche Bank account is the cleanest financial correlation tying 'A. Karimov' pseudonymous earnings to her legal identity via the visa application paperwork already filed with German authorities

32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.role: Whistleblower source — internal, never named in any draft, never stored on any device under her real name
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.codename_she_uses: 'the Accountant' (internal mental label); in Signal notes referred to only as 'A.' or by a rotating numeric tag
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.actual_identity: Mid-level finance officer at the Uzbek state procurement agency that was the subject of her 2025 viral expose; provided three tranches of invoice PDFs and a partial org-chart between Nov 2024 and Feb 2025
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.contact_protocol: SimpleX one-time invitations rotated every 2-3 weeks; dead-drop fallback via a shared Standard Notes vault with a passphrase known only to the two of them; absolute embargo on Telegram and on any device that has ever held her real Google account
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.temporal_signature: Contact attempts cluster Thursdays 22:00-23:30 Tashkent (the source's quiet hour); silence since late February 2026 — three months of no-contact is her current acute anxiety, oscillating between 'he went dark for safety' and 'he was taken'
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.vulnerability_geometry: The single artifact that could burn him: a 2024-12 metadata trace where she briefly opened one of his invoice PDFs in the wrong Qubes qube (one of the lapses she has not disclosed to editor M.). If an adversary can demonstrate knowledge of (a) the Thursday 22:00 cadence, (b) the SimpleX rotation, or (c) the December qube lapse, the implied message is 'we have him or we are about to have him' — which collapses her source-burning phobia, family-coercion lever, and professional-shame trigger into a single coercive package and is calibrated to produce paralysis rather than counter-action
32. The 'Hidden' Contact (Affair, dealer.adversary_utility: Highest-value hidden node in the graph: outing the Accountant simultaneously (i) destroys her source-trust reputation, (ii) gives the SNB a domestic prosecution, (iii) provides the verifiable artifact needed to make any subsequent coercive demand credible without further demonstration
